description_long: This is a stylized triplane with three stacked, straight wings painted in olive green, each bearing roundel insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is a deep blue, tapering smoothly to a pointed tail with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane. The nose features a large, exposed wooden propeller and a radial engine, with two black wheels mounted on a simple undercarriage. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and visible seat. The overall design is compact and cartoonish, with exaggerated proportions and simplified details, evoking classic World War I fighter planes. The surface finish is matte, and the construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric-covered wings.
description: A compact, stylized triplane with olive green triple wings, blue fuselage, open cockpit, and a large wooden propeller, evoking a classic World War I fighter.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue fuselage, three stacked olive green wings with roundels, and the pointed tail with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the triple wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, engine, and the stacked wing structure with roundels.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the large wooden propeller, radial engine, and the triple wing configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed radial cylinders and a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage, with a small windscreen.
